paper_title: Experimental Design and Resharpening Process in Schöningen

methods.description: This method involves producing and using scraper replicas to analyze the microdebitage produced by their resharpening, simulating the activities of Schöningen hominins.

steps.description: One of the authors (RW) produced the experimental scrapers using high-quality, fine-grained Baltic flint collected on the coast of northern Germany.
steps.searchable_summary: produce scraper replicas using Baltic flint

steps.description: The scrapers were manually used by FV for a minimum of 60 minutes for scraping tasks, except for one tool used by combining transversal and longitudinal motions for 30 minutes.
We observed the distribution of use-related residues on the scraper's active edges prior to resharpening at low magnifications.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze use-related residues on scraper edges

methods.description: This method describes the resharpening of used scrapers and the collection of resharpening flakes to study technological microtraces and residues.

steps.description: The flintknapper RW resharpened the used scrapers by recreating the original scalar retouch identified on some archeological specimens.
We designed the experiments with the aim of testing soft and hard hammer percussion.
steps.searchable_summary: test soft and hard hammer percussion techniques

steps.description: We produced a total of 152 resharpening flakes on a total of 7 tools.
Each flake was then placed in a clean plastic bag, awaiting microscopic analysis.
